# PNA Spec

> Universal specification for personal network applications (PNAs). Defines vocabulary, goals, use cases, axes, composition (how PNAs get built), architectural commitments, and slot contracts. Targeted at AI agents and humans building PNAs together.

## Skill (agent entry point)

- [pna-build-eval-contrib/SKILL.md](pna-build-eval-contrib/SKILL.md) — canonical PNT skill for AI agents. Three flows: build a conformant PNA from the spec, evaluate a candidate PNA's conformance against the spec, contribute a design back to PNT
- [docs/users-guide.md](docs/users-guide.md) — human-facing step-by-step instructions for each flow
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) — how reference designs (and any spec changes they motivate) get accepted into PNT

## Spec

- [PNA Spec](spec/PNA_Spec.md) — universal spec; Spec-Version 0.1 (draft)
- [Axes](spec/axes.md) — the Axes a PNA varies along, attested picks per Axis, and the flavor-derived ACs each pick triggers
- [Use cases](spec/use_cases.md) — attested classes of PNA (Directory Archive, Personal Relationship Manager [draft], Multi-PNA ecosystem [target v0.2+])
- [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md) — spec version history

## Typed contracts

- [contracts/](contracts/) — JSON Schema, OpenAPI fragments, SQL DDL, and TypeScript declarations for the load-bearing interfaces (worker handshake, distribution auth, comms transport, shared/private DB schemas, five canonical MCP servers — Shared Data Ops, Private Data Ops, Ingestion, Communications, Diagnostics)

## Reference designs

- [fellows_local_db Architecture](https://github.com/richbodo/fellows_local_db/blob/main/docs/Architecture.md) — first reference design; Directory Archive use case; magic-link distributed PWA + static network DB archive + single shared directory
